activities_projects.title: Habitat Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Identify biotic and abiotic components of habitats
steps: Identify two habitats around you such as a pond, forest, farm, or large tree
Observe living organisms and non-living things in each habitat
Record them in two columns as biotic and abiotic components
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that habitats contain both living and non-living components

activities_projects.title: Population Counting Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of population in a habitat
steps: Mark a 1 m × 1 m area in a garden or field
Identify two plants and two animals in that area
Count the number of individuals of each organism
Record the data in a table
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how populations are measured in ecology

activities_projects.title: Food Chain Identification
steps: List organisms found in a grassland ecosystem such as grass, grasshopper, frog, snake, eagle
Arrange them in order of 'who eats whom'
Draw arrows to show energy flow
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the concept of a food chain

activities_projects.title: Food Web Diagram
activities_projects.objective: Understand how food chains connect to form food webs
steps: List at least six organisms from a forest or grassland ecosystem
Draw multiple feeding relationships among them
Connect the food chains to form a food web
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand complex feeding relationships in ecosystems
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and correctness of diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Producer and Consumer Classification
activities_projects.objective: Classify organisms based on their feeding habits
steps: List organisms such as tree, deer, fox, mushroom, bird, squirrel
Classify them into producers, herbivores, carnivores, and omnivores
Explain their role in the ecosystem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand ecological roles of organisms

activities_projects.title: Decomposer Observation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of decomposers in nature
steps: Observe places where dead leaves or organic matter collect
Identify organisms such as fungi, mushrooms, or insects
Explain how they help recycle nutrients
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand decomposition and nutrient recycling

activities_projects.title: Ecosystem Interaction Study
activities_projects.objective: Identify interactions among organisms and environment
steps: Observe an ecosystem such as a pond or garden
List interactions between organisms (e.g., frog eats insects)
List interactions between organisms and environment (e.g., fish in water)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand biotic and abiotic interactions

activities_projects.title: Symbiotic Relationship Examples
steps: Find examples of mutualism, commensalism, and parasitism
Write the organisms involved in each case
Explain the benefit or harm in each relationship

activities_projects.title: Ecosystem Benefits Research
steps: List at least five benefits provided by ecosystems
Explain how forests, rivers, and wetlands support human life
Write a short paragraph about ecosystem conservation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect ecosystems with human well-being

activities_projects.title: Farming Practices Survey
activities_projects.objective: Understand sustainable and modern farming practices
steps: Visit a nearby farm or interact with a farmer
Ask questions about fertilizers, pesticides, and farming methods
Record the responses and prepare a short report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about sustainable agriculture and environmental impact
